seo.title: Building Customer Support with Smolagents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with Smolagents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with Smolagents leverages its lightweight, code-first approach for fast RAG-based reasoning and tool integration with CRMs. However, the simplicity that makes Smolagents powerful also exposes risks like unsafe code generation and multi-step reasoning failures in high-stakes support scenarios. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify response accuracy, filter PII, ensure policy compliance, and maintain professional tone.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with Smolagents
architectureOverview.description:
A typical Smolagents customer support agent uses a code agent for RAG retrieval from a knowledge base, combined with tool agents for CRM operations like ticket creation and order lookup. The agent receives user queries, retrieves relevant docs via vector search, reasons over context with an LLM, and calls tools to update Salesforce or Zendesk. Multi-step flows handle escalations by checking sentiment and routing to human agents when needed.

The architecture emphasizes minimal boilerplate: a single agent.run() orchestrates retrieval, reasoning, and action. Knowledge bases are indexed via simple embedding functions, while tools are defined with Pydantic schemas for type safety. This enables rapid prototyping but requires verification at runtime to catch compounding errors in complex support interactions.

Vex wraps the agent execution to validate outputs against support-specific criteria like PII redaction and escalation triggers before responses reach customers.

risks.name: Unsafe Python Execution
risks.description: Code agent generates malicious or resource-intensive Python like os.system() calls or infinite loops during ticket processing
risks.mitigation: Wrap agent.run() with Vex sandboxed execution and code safety checks

risks.name: PII Leakage in Responses
risks.description: Agent exposes customer phone numbers, emails, or order details pulled from CRM without redaction
risks.mitigation: Vex PII filtering scans all outputs before customer delivery

risks.name: Tone Inconsistency
risks.description: Multi-step reasoning produces unprofessional, sarcastic, or overly casual responses compounding across conversation
risks.mitigation: Vex tone verification against brand voice guidelines per response

risks.name: Missed Escalation Triggers
risks.description: Agent fails to detect urgent keywords (refund, fraud, outage) and continues self-service flow inappropriately
risks.mitigation: Vex policy checks for escalation patterns and required human handoff

implementation.title: RAG Knowledge Base Setup
implementation.description: Index support docs with sentence embeddings for fast retrieval in agent reasoning
implementation.code:
from smolagents import CodeAgent
from smolagents.tools import tool
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
import faiss

# Index support knowledge base
model = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')
docs = ['Returns policy...', 'Shipping FAQ...', 'Billing support...']
embeddings = model.encode(docs)
index = faiss.IndexFlatL2(embeddings.shape[1])
index.add(embeddings.astype('float32'))

@tool
def retrieve_docs(query: str) -> str:
    query_emb = model.encode([query])
    _, indices = index.search(query_emb.astype('float32'), 3)
    return '\n'.join([docs[i] for i in indices[0]])

implementation.title: CRM Tool Definitions
implementation.description: Define typed tools for ticket creation and order lookup with Pydantic validation

implementation.title: Core Support Agent
implementation.description: Combine RAG retrieval with CRM tools in single code agent
implementation.code:
agent = CodeAgent(
    model="mistral-7b-instruct",
    tools=[retrieve_docs, create_ticket, lookup_order],
    instructions="""You are a helpful customer support agent.
    1. Retrieve relevant docs first
    2. Use tools for CRM actions
    3. Escalate critical issues
    4. Keep responses professional and concise"""
)

def handle_support_query(query: str) -> str:
    return agent.run(query)

implementation.title: Escalation Detection Logic
implementation.description: Multi-step reasoning to detect and handle escalation triggers

vexIntegration.title: Basic Vex Wrapper for agent.run()
vexIntegration.description: Wrap core agent execution with comprehensive verification suite
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify

def secure_support_handler(query: str) -> str:
    raw_response = handle_support_query(query)
    
    verified = verify(
        raw_response,
        checks=[
            "response_accuracy",
            "pii_filtering",
            "tone_consistency:professional",
            "policy_compliance:support",
            "escalation_triggers"
        ],
        context={"query": query}
    )
    return verified.response

vexIntegration.title: PII + Tone Verification
vexIntegration.description: Specific checks for customer support compliance requirements

vexIntegration.title: Escalation + Policy Guardrails
vexIntegration.description: Policy enforcement with automatic escalation for high-risk queries
vexIntegration.code:
from vex.policies import SupportPolicy

policy = SupportPolicy(
    escalation_keywords=["fraud", "legal", "refund dispute"],
    max_autoresolve_steps=3,
    pii_redaction=True
)

result = verify(
    raw_response,
    policy=policy,
    context={
        "customer_tier": "premium",
        "conversation_history": history,
        "query_sentiment": sentiment_score
    }
)

if result.should_escalate:
    return f"Transferring to specialist: {result.escalation_reason}"

cta.heading: Secure Your Smolagents Customer Support
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your agent.run() calls to eliminate support risks immediately.
